Act III — The Regulatory Tax
A Tale of Two Worlds
"Same brand, same shelf space, half the volume. The flavor ban isn't an abstraction — it's a measurable revenue cap."
Flavor Policy Impact
Texas vs California: The Flavor Ban Tax
92% store parity. 56% of the volume. The only variable: Prop 31.

Per-Store Volume Gap
CA: 85 vs TX: 139 — same brand, different laws
~154K
Lost Volume (Annual Est.)
If CA matched TX efficiency: 394K − 240K
92%
Store Parity
Distribution is there. Volume isn't. Flavor is the variable.

California is the clearest proof point in the portfolio. 92% of Texas's store footprint, 56% of the volume. The only structural difference: Prop 31 limits FRE to a single unflavored SKU at retail. That's not a distribution problem — it's a regulatory volume cap. And yet CA is still #2, still growing at 42% YoY. The demand is there. The law is the ceiling.

429K · 3,084 stores

The growth map reveals two stories: The Mountain West corridor (NV, UT, CO, ID, NM) is exploding from a small base — these are new-market entries gaining traction fast. Meanwhile, Florida and Texas are doing something harder: growing 90%+ YoY on top of already-massive volume. FL at 99K volume with +102% growth is arguably the single most exciting market in the portfolio. Every top-growth state is flavor-allowed.

The 6mg Flywheel

6mg consumers are the most loyal, most valuable, most flavor-dependent buyers in the portfolio — and flavor policy is the single biggest lever determining whether FRE reaches them at scale.

Sources: MSA Scanner (L52W ending 03/29/2026) · Haypp Q3 2024 Repeat Purchase Data · Shopify DTC (Jan 1 – Apr 1, 2026) · FRE Internal Sales 2025–2026
Note: 6mg cross-buy analysis: 7,976 DTC orders containing 6mg product. Retention = % returning customers. AOV = average order value. State volume = MSA scanner-equivalent units.
California: Prop 31 (effective Dec 2022) restricts sale of flavored tobacco products. Only unflavored/tobacco pouches available at CA retail.
3D Renders: PyVista + VTK with Phong shading, Census Bureau state boundaries, volume-scaled extrusion. Growth map: height + color = YoY change.
